comments: What happens if the community consistently votes for pools with low TVL? Doesn't that create a negative feedback loop? The ve(3,3) model looks interesting but how does it prevent vote manipulation from larger holders? Weekly emissions sound great but how does MarbMarket handle potential governance apathy from small token holders? No VC backing is impressive but what's the plan for sustainable revenue when the initial hype dies down?
What happens when bigger projects start offering bigger bribes? Won't that just concentrate power in the hands of a few wealthy holders?
Seems interesting but how does this prevent governance attacks where whales could manipulate the vote outcomes for personal gain?
If there's no presale and it's truly fair, how are the initial tokens distributed to ensure early liquidity?
This actually sounds like a game-changer - giving real power to the community rather than just paying lip service to decentralization.
The longer lock periods for more rewards sounds great but doesn't that discourage short-term liquidity providers from participating?
This veDEX model is interesting. How does the bribe mechanism work in practice?
Fair launch with no VCs is the way to go. What chain has the most veDEX activity right now?
The lock duration mechanic creates good incentive alignment. Would love to see emission data after launch.
How does MarbMarket compare to Aerodrome on Base in terms of actual TVL and user activity?
Vote-escrow models need real participation. What happens if voter turnout is low in early weeks?
MegaETH speed is a real advantage here. Sub-second finality for governance votes is huge.
What is the minimum MARB needed to participate meaningfully in governance?
Bribing in ve(3,3) sounds negative but is actually positive sum. Most people miss that.
How will MarbMarket attract initial liquidity without VC backing or incentives pre-launch?
The fair launch narrative is strong but execution matters more. Any audits announced?
Curious about the team behind this. Any previous DeFi builds they have shipped?
Lock duration determines your power. That is smart for long-term alignment but rough for short-term traders.
What happens to veMARB voting power as the unlock period approaches? Does it decay linearly?
Compared to Solidly, what improvements has MarbMarket made to the base ve(3,3) model?
content:
Been seeing so many DEX launches lately that feel the same. Then I found MarbMarket - first veDEX on MegaETH with no VC money. That's a rare thing these days.

What makes veDEX different? It's simple. You lock MARB tokens to get voting power. Longer lock = more influence + more rewards. Weekly votes decide which LP pools get emissions. Projects pay bribes for votes. That's the MARB incentive loop in action.

Most DEXs just throw rewards at random. MarbMarket lets the community decide where incentives go. Fair launch, no presale. That changes everything for liquidity mining on MegaETH.

x.com/Marb_market

What's your take on governance-driven incentive models? Do they actually work better than random emissions? @RallyOnChain
